title: zanthoxylum arnottianum subsp. alatum
intro: Zanthoxylum arnottianum subsp. alatum, commonly known as the Kaua'i island prickly ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Hawaii. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum arnottianum subsp. alatum include reducing pain and inflammation, improving respiratory health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or oil, and can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
